market_context: US stock futures are mixed to lower amid ongoing Middle East tensions, with Trump's 10-day delay on Iran strikes providing some relief but oil surging to $103-$110/barrel keeping pressure on markets. Nasdaq entered correction territory after Thursday's rout, while Dow, S&P 500, and Nasdaq futures show declines of 0.1-0.6% in latest updates. High Treasury yields around 4.41% and Fed speakers today add to caution.

predictions.reasoning: S&P 500 futures are down 0.4% per latest updates amid surging oil prices above $110/barrel and persistent geopolitical risks despite Trump's strike delay[1][3]. Nasdaq correction and Citi's neutral equity stance signal broad pressure[2][3]. Elevated 10-year yields at 4.41% further weigh on equities[4].

Nasdaq 100 futures down 0.6% reflect correction territory after Thursday rout, exacerbated by oil at $107+ and war uncertainties[2][3]. Prediction markets show ~62% odds for 23900 level, implying modest downside from current futures[6]. Tech sensitivity to higher yields and Fed hike odds (~36-50%) adds downside risk[2][7].

Dow futures fell 0.3-160 points (some reports up to 469) on oil surge and mixed Trump-Iran news[3][5]. Energy disruptions risk tempers relief from strike delay[1][2]. Broader caution from delayed PCE data and Fed comments supports limited downside[2].

Brent crude up 2% above $110 and WTI over $96 amid Strait of Hormuz concerns and Iran tensions[1][3]. Trump's delay eases immediate supply fears but unrest sustains upward momentum[1][2]. Oil volatility dominates market narrative[2][3].
